response.justification: Bogdan has a solid 15+ year engineering career with genuine breadth across web, mobile, infrastructure, and embedded systems, and his current Google role touches AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling. However, the role demands Director-level strategic seniority, recognized AI expertise, and the ability to advise enterprise customers on cloud/AI adoption — none of which are evident from the resume. His AI exposure is recent (since April 2025) and tactical rather than strategic, he holds only bachelor's degrees with no advanced AI-related education, and there is no evidence of the executive-level influence, thought leadership, or customer-facing advisory experience this principal-level AI strategy role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of continuous software engineering experience spanning multiple domains (web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure, data)
Current role at Google involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, showing direct engagement with agentic AI tooling
Demonstrated entrepreneurial instinct through founding an open-source geo-data platform, organizing community workshops, and delivering conference talks
concerns: No advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in AI or a related technical field; holds only two bachelor's degrees, one in Fine Arts
AI expertise is shallow and very recent — limited to ~1 year at Google with no demonstrated AI products or platforms built and scaled at industry-recognized scope

No evidence of Director-level scope: no strategic customer advisory work, no cross-org influence, no published AI research or thought leadership that matches the seniority and visibility the role demands

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. director-level IC requirement
